body:
﻿NEEDS_CORRECTION (doc-only stale prose).

The code shape looks right: the retired variants are gone from live references, `EditorKeyCommand` is time-scale-only, and the re-anchored guard now asserts the five File/Edit binds are menu-routed while `from_key_press` returns `None`. I did not find a functional blocker.

But the stale-prose sweep is incomplete. Several live comments still describe the pre-W08.4 shadow map:

- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs:6-8` still says `EditorKeyCommand` is the closed enum for `Ctrl+Z` / `Ctrl+Y` / `Ctrl+S`; it is now the time-scale-only `Ctrl+2/0/4` enum.
- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:2219-2223` still frames the `window_event` flow as "after the Ctrl-bound `EditorKeyCommand` lookup" for playback, and `:2237-2238` says the parity guard keeps `from_key_press` and the menu in lockstep. Post-W08.4 the menu path is first and `from_key_press` is only the time-scale fallback / no-shadow assertion.
- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:2259-2262` still says the Save / Save-As / Undo / Redo variants are shadowed "until W08.4 retires them"; this PR is W08.4, so that future-tense note is now false.
- `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs:384-386` says the parity guard locks the keyboard map to the menu. It now pins the menu bindings plus absence of an `EditorKeyCommand` shadow.
- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/playback.rs:31-33` still says "Ctrl-bound bindings -> `commands::EditorKeyCommand` -> bus". That should be narrowed to time-scale-only, with File/Edit accelerators going through the canonical menu path.
- `crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu_tests.rs:194-200` still says File/Edit accelerator displays mirror live routing via `EditorKeyCommand` + the Ctrl+O arm. After W08.4 they mirror the canonical menu source itself; there is no File/Edit `EditorKeyCommand` mirror or inline Ctrl+O arm.

The dated state-doc/history hits can remain, and the intentional prose saying W08.4 retired the old arm is fine. This request is only about live crate/test comments that now describe the wrong current architecture.

Fixed in `839b281` (comment/doc only — no code/test logic change).

You're right — my W08.4 sweep grepped for the retired *symbols* (`EditorKeyCommand::Undo` etc.) but missed the *conceptual* shadow-map prose. Corrected all 5 flagged files:

- **commands.rs** — module-header `EditorKeyCommand` set (Ctrl+Z/Y/S → the Ctrl+2/0/4 time-scale binds); the `undo_command`/`redo_command`/`submit_action` docs now point the `NothingToUndo`/`NothingToRedo` swallow at `route_menu_command`, not the retired `handle_key_command` arms.
- **lifecycle/mod.rs** — the `window_event` playback fall-through comment reflects the new ladder order (menu lookup → EditorKeyCommand time-scale → playback); dropped the "shadowed … until W08.4 retires them" wording.
- **render_path.rs** — `route_menu_command` doc + the Edit Undo/Redo arm comment no longer frame the menu as "behaviour-identical to the Ctrl+Z/Y bus path / exactly as `handle_key_command` does" (the keystrokes resolve *through* this method now).
- **playback.rs** — module-header dispatch-lane description + the test-coverage reference (`keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs` drives `route_menu_command`).
- **menu_tests.rs** — the accelerator-display comment (menu is the canonical source, not a mirror of `EditorKeyCommand`).

A whole-class re-grep also caught **2 more** in `menu_tests.rs` your sample didn't list — two `"no accelerator display yet (deferred with W08 execution)"` assertion messages → Play uses the plain Space/Escape PIE binds (W08 is done; Play has no menu accelerator by design).

Dated history (mod.rs `# 2026-05-21 Phase 9 …` section) left as-is per your dated-references-stay note. A final re-grep for the stale-framing class returns no live matches. `fmt --check` clean; editor-shell (226 lib + integration) + editor-egui-host tests pass; CI re-running the full gate on `839b281`.

﻿NEEDS_CORRECTION (doc-only, round 2).

Most of the stale shadow-map prose is fixed, but two live comments still describe the old W08.2/W08.3 parity model:

- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:2236-2237` still says the parity guard keeps `from_key_press` and the menu "in lockstep." Post-W08.4, File/Edit intentionally are *not* in lockstep with `from_key_press`: the menu maps Ctrl+O/S/Shift+S/Z/Y to commands, while `from_key_press` returns `None` for all five so no shadow table remains.
- `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs:384-386` still says the parity guard locks the keyboard map to the same menu. That should be narrowed to the post-W08.4 invariant: the live keyboard path and menu item both route through `Command`, while the accelerator guard pins the canonical menu bindings plus absence of an `EditorKeyCommand` shadow.

The remaining `save_request.rs` mention of the retired `EditorKeyCommand::Save` arm is fine because it explicitly says it was retired; dated state-doc/history hits can also remain. This is just these two live comments that still phrase the guard as a `from_key_press <-> menu` agreement.

Fixed in `8e48b43` (comment-only).

Both "lockstep" comments corrected — post-W08.4 the invariant isn't "two maps kept in sync," it's "menu is canonical + `from_key_press → None` for the five File/Edit binds, so no shadow remains":

- **lifecycle/mod.rs:2236** — "the parity guard keeps `from_key_press` and the menu in lockstep" → "pins that the menu binds these five accelerators while `from_key_press` returns None for them (no shadow remains)".
- **render_path.rs:384** — "the parity guard locks the keyboard map to the same menu …" → a keystroke and its menu item dispatch the identical `Command` because both resolve through the same canonical menu; the guard pins that `from_key_press` no longer shadows these binds.

A whole-class re-grep (`lockstep` / `locks the keyboard` / `in sync` / `agree` / `diverge`) finds no other stale parity-framing in editor-shell. `fmt --check` clean; CI re-running on `8e48b43`. (And agreed — the `save_request.rs:7` retired-arm mention is intentional prose.)

﻿APPROVED (content).

Re-review of `8e48b43` clears the stale-prose findings:

- The explicit old phrases (`from_key_press`/menu "lockstep", "locks the keyboard map", `EditorKeyCommand + Ctrl+O arm`, future-tense "until W08.4 retires", etc.) no longer appear in the live crate/test comments.
- The corrected comments now state the post-W08.4 invariant: File/Edit accelerators live in the canonical menu, `from_key_press` intentionally returns `None` for those five binds, and `EditorKeyCommand` remains only for the time-scale execution-only keys.
- The remaining retired-symbol mention in `save_request.rs` is accurate prose saying the old `EditorKeyCommand::Save` arm was retired.

Functional review remains clear from the prior pass: retired variants are gone from live code references, tests are migrated to `route_menu_command`, the re-anchored guard pins menu bindings + absence of the shadow table, scope is the expected 13 files after the doc cleanup, and `diff --check` is clean.

CI was still waiting on CodeQL Rust when checked, so this is content-approved with the usual CI + human-go hold.
